item.title: Supply snags keep most petrol pumps shut
item.description: Most of the filling stations in the capital Dhaka on Wednesday remained closed or operated for limited period of time, leading consumers to crowd a few open pumps amid a fuel oil crisis in the country because of the war in the Gulf......

item.title: ICT investigators find ex-IGP Mamun’s involvement in Shapla Square crackdown
item.description: The chief prosecutor of the International Crimes Tribunal, Md Aminul Islam, said on Wednesday that investigators had found the involvement of former inspector general of police Chowdhury Abdullah Al Mamun in the 2013 crackdown on Hefazat-e-Islam Bangladesh’s sit-in at Shapla Chattar in the capital......Maps

item.title: Recruitment of five lakh public employees under way
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday told Jatiya Sangsad that initiatives were taken to sign bilateral agreements to expand alternative labour markets in new countries, including Serbia, Greece, North Macedonia, Romania, Portugal, Brazil and Russia......

item.title: Learning from previous progs stressed for quality pry edn
item.description: The government should assess the impact of the long-term projects taken for the primary education before taking new ones, said educationists and researchers......

item.title: Lawmakers debate path to implement July Charter
item.description: Lawmakers engaged in a debate in Jatiya Sangsad on Wednesday, focusing on the implementation of the July National Charter, constitutional reform, and governance issues......

item.title: PM seeks $2b from dev partners for energy
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday sought $2 billion fund from development partners to meet the Bangladesh’s immediate energy needs and safeguard its economic stability......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Urea shortage feared as production, imports stall
item.description: The country may face a shortage of urea fertiliser in the upcoming Aman season as imports remain stalled amid the US–Iran war and domestic production remains suspended due to a persistent gas crisis......

item.title: Measles cases keep surging
item.description: Measles cases continue to surge across Bangladesh, with a 50 per cent test positivity rate recorded among samples collected at Bangladesh Shishu Hospital and Institute, raising fresh concerns among health experts over the scale of the outbreak......

item.title: Confronting trafficking route to Europe
item.description: IN THE Mediterranean Sea, somewhere between Tobruk and Crete, as many as 18 Bangladeshi young men out of 22 watched their hope die, one body at a time, over six days at sea. Among the 26 rescued survivors, 21 Bangladeshi men said they were forced to throw their peers’ bodies into the water during the ordeal. In total, 43 out of 48 people involved in this journey were Bangladeshi......

item.title: Crossing diplomatic minefield
item.description: THE United States and Iran began Pakistan-mediated negotiations on April 11 to strike a permanent ceasefire, ending the hostilities that the United States and Israel initiated against Iran on February 28. A permanent negotiated ceasefire between the United States and Iran is so profoundly complex and delicate that it resides on the edge of impossibility. The structural......

item.title: Reshaping Bangladesh’s agriculture
item.description: ON A winter morning in Bangladesh’s north, a rice farmer checks his mobile before switching on the irrigation pump. The message that he reads is not from a neighbour or a local trader. It is generated from satellite data orbiting hundreds of kilometres above the earth. It tells him exactly how much water his field needs. In a country where agriculture still employs nearly......

item.title: Measles exposes health security gaps
item.description: BANGLADESH is in the grip of a lethal measles outbreak that has already claimed dozens of young lives and left thousands more infected. At least 118 children have died since mid-March 2026, while hospitals continue to be inundated with mostly young patients. The timing is particularly alarming: 2026 had been set as the target year for measles elimination in the......

item.title: Road safety, condition warrant early oversight
item.description: VEHICLE modification and overloading remain two major threats to road safety and road conditions in the absence of almost any oversight. Vehicle modification, which is done for overloading, and overloading without any modification are rampant. The authorities, as New Age reported on April 14, are said often to be giving in to the pressure of transport owners......

item.title: Making critical care functional counts
item.description: IT IS unacceptable that half of the intensive care capacity has remained unused, rendering the post-Covid expansion unsuccessful. During and after the Covid outbreak, the authorities undertook a project to expand the intensive care capacity, nearly tripling it. The country now has 1,372 ICU beds in 72 public facilities, almost three times the pre-Covid total of 498......

item.title: Arsenal survive tense Sporting stalemate to reach Champions League semis
Bayern sink Real Madrid late to reach Champions League semis
item.description: Late goals from Luis Diaz and Michael Olise earned Bayern Munich a 4-3 win over Real Madrid on Wednesday, securing a 6-4 aggregate victory and sending them through to face holders Paris Saint-Germain in the semi-finals....

item.title: Pry scholarship exams start in 61 dists
item.description: Primary scholarship examinations for 2025 began on Wednesday in 61 districts despite criticism from the guardians and educationists......

item.title: PM opens pre-piloting farmer card
item.description: The prime minister, Tarique Rahman, on Tuesday inaugurated the ‘Farmer Card’ distribution in 10 districts across Bangladesh in the pre-piloting phase as part of the implementation of the ruling BNP’s election pledges......Politics analysis

item.title: Jamaat brings out Baishakhi Shobhajatra
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Tuesday held the Baishakhi Shobhajatra, a traditional procession, in the capital Dhaka to mark Pahela Baishakh, the first day of the Bengali New Year......

item.title: Professor Abdus Salam takes charge as DU pro-VC
item.description: Professor Abdus Salam of the department of chemistry took charge as pro-vice-chancellor, academic, of the University of Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Universities welcome Bengali new year
item.description: The University of Chittagong, the Rabindra University, Bangladesh, the Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology and the Gazipur Agricultural University welcomed Bengali new year 1433 through colourful programmes, including Baishakhi fair, procession, children’s painting competition and cultural performances on the campuses of the universities on Tuesday......

item.title: Many fishermen become unemployed as 58-day ban on fishing in Bay begins
item.description: A 58-day government ban on all types of fishing in Bangladeshi waters of the Bay of Bengal has begun......

item.title: Family receives expat’s body returned from Bahrain
item.description: The body of Girish Chandra Sutradhar, a Bangladeshi expatriate who passed away while working in Bahrain, was repatriated to Bangladesh Wednesday morning......Maps

item.title: Attendance discrepancies reported in GST admission test results
item.description: Allegations of statistical inconsistencies have surfaced in the published results of the GST (general, science and technology) cluster admission test for the 2025–26 academic session under the ‘A’ (Science) unit, with 53 candidates reportedly shown present despite not appearing in the examination......

item.title: ACC seizes, freezes assets worth Tk 28.5cr in March
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission seized and froze movable and immovable assets worth Tk 28,51,80,113 in March following 12 court orders......

item.title: 13 killed in road accidents in 2 days
item.description: At least 13 people were killed and 10 more were injured in road crashes in Nilphamari, Cumilla, Barguna and Khulna districts between Tuesday and Wednesday......

item.title: ‘Serial rapist’ arrested, police trace 10 incidents
item.description: The police have arrested a man on charges of raping and blackmailing young women after snaring them into friendship on social media......

item.title: Bangladeshis among 300 migrants rescued in Greece
item.description: Greek authorities have rescued over 300 migrants coming mainly from Bangladesh, Egypt, Yemen and Sudan over the last two days, officials said Tuesday......

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut, Hezbollah launches rockets
item.description: Two Israeli strikes on Wednesday hit vehicles south of Beirut, state media reported, while Hezbollah launched rockets at Israel, hours after Lebanon and Israel agreed to hold direct negotiations......

item.title: Zelensky, Italian PM pledge defence, drone cooperation
item.description: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni and Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky said Wednesday they would work together to boost defence cooperation, including on drone production, after talks in Rome......

item.title: Civic bodies demand policy to regulate doctors’ fees
item.description: Two civic organisations on Wednesday called for an effective policy to regulate consultation fees for specialist physicians in Rajshahi, alleging that rising charges were preventing many patients from seeking treatment from specialist doctors......

item.title: Arms dealer Helal on 3-day remand
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday placed Majedul Haque Helal, who allegedly supplied the weapon used in the murder of Inquilab Mancha convener Sharif Osman Hadi, on a three-day remand in a case......

item.title: Rohingya youth loses foot in landmine blast
item.description: A Rohingya youth lost the foot of his right leg in a landmine explosion near the Ukhiya border area of Cox’s Bazar on Wednesday morning......

item.title: Hearing against Nanak, Taposh, 26 others deferred to April 26
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Wednesday deferred until April 26 the charge-framing hearing in a case over alleged crimes against humanity committed in Mohammadpur during the July uprising against 28 accused, including former minister Jahangir Kabir Nanak and former DSCC mayor Sheikh Fazle Noor Taposh......

item.title: Chinese counsellor meets BMU VC
item.description: Chinese cultural counsellor Li Sharpen and third secretary Zhu Yong met with Bangladesh Medical University vice-chancellor Professor Dr FM Siddiqui at the BMU in Dhaka on Wednesday......Politics analysis

item.title: Jamaat, Khelafat Majlish leaders among 200 sued
item.description: A case has been filed against four identified individuals, including two local leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and the Bangladesh Khelafat Majlish, along with 180 to 200 unidentified people over the April 11 killing of pir Abdur Rahman in a mob attack on his durbar sharif in Daulatpur upazila of Kushtia......

item.title: Police hid bullet wounds in my son’s inquest report, cop tells ICT
item.description: The father of Imam Hasan Taim, himself a police officer, told the International Crimes Tribunal on Wednesday that his son was shot dead in the capital’s Jatrabari area during the July 2024 uprising, but fellow officers concealed evidence of bullet injuries in the inquest report......

item.title: Govt plans e-health cards rollout in five districts: PM
item.description: The government took an initiative to introduce e-health cards in five districts, prime minister Tarique Rahman told Jatiya Sangsad on Wednesday......

item.title: Govt plans English training for 2.47 lakh primary teachers: PM
item.description: The government has planned to implement a five-year Fifth Primary Education Development Programme, beginning on July 1, to provide English language training to around 2.47 lakh primary school teachers, prime minister Tarique Rahman told Jatiya Sangsad on Wednesday......

item.title: Industries face credit crisis amid high liquidity: DCCI
item.description: Domestic industries and private businesses in Bangladesh are struggling to access bank credit despite ample liquidity, as rising default risks and heavy government borrowing continue to squeeze lending to the productive sector, according to Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry......

item.title: Energy shortage to reduce Bangladesh’s cotton import
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture has again lowered its projection for Bangladesh’s cotton imports to 7.7 million bales for the ongoing 2025–26 marketing year, citing persistent energy supply shortages......

item.title: RMG makers urge govt to continue policy support
item.description: The country’s readymade garment manufacturers on Wednesday urged the government to continue strong policy support to maintain the competitiveness of the country’s apparel sector amid mounting global economic challenges......Maps

item.title: NBR extends VAT return deadline
item.description: The National Board of Revenue on Wednesday extended the deadline for submitting online Value Added Tax returns for the month of March of this year......

item.title: BB buys $70m from banks
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has bought nearly $5.56 billion from commercial banks since July 2025 amid stronger inflows......

item.title: Broadband expo signals shift toward homegrown tech
item.description: The three-day ‘Broadband Expo 2026’ in Dhaka concluded on Wednesday, showcasing faster internet solutions and emphasising Bangladesh’s shift from foreign technology dependency to fostering homegrown innovation in its digital landscape......

item.title: BEPZA enters 46th year
item.description: Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority has entered the 46th year of its operations on Wednesday since its formal inception in 1981......

item.title: Cox’s Bazar airport to be ready for int’l flights in few months: Afroza
item.description: Civil aviation and tourism minister Afroza Khanam on Wednesday told the Parliament that the upgradation of Cox’s Bazar Airport into an international airport would take a few more months......

item.title: Gopalganj farmers embrace bitter gourd cultivation for higher profits
item.description: Farmers in Gopalganj are increasingly turning to bitter gourd cultivation, drawn by the high profit margins and relatively low cost of production......

item.title: Global stocks steadier on Mideast peace hopes
item.description: Markets largely steadied Wednesday as traders waited to see if Mideast peace talks resume, though share prices of Gucci parent Kering and French luxury group Hermes tumbled on weak earnings linked partly to the conflict’s fallout......

item.title: Bangladesh, Turkey move to strengthen trade, investment ties
item.description: Turkish ambassador to Bangladesh Ramis Sen called on commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Wednesday at the Secretariat to discuss expanding bilateral trade, increasing investment and strengthening economic cooperation between the two nations......Bangladesh travel

item.title: Stocks revert to positive territory
item.description: Stocks on Wednesday reverted to positive territory after the previous session’s decline, as opportunistic investors stepped in for bargain hunting while remaining cautiously attuned to evolving situation surrounding the ceasefire talks in the Middle East conflict......

item.title: Norway oil exports hit record high
item.description: Norway’s crude oil exports hit a record high in March as the war in Iran and closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz shipping lane drove up oil prices, Statistics Norway said Wednesday......

item.title: Pubali Bank opens electronic banking booth at NIBPS
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC on Wednesday inaugurated its electronic banking booth at National Institute of Burn and Plastic Surgery in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Community Bank holds 74th board meeting
item.description: Community Bank Bangladesh PLC on Monday held its 74th board meeting at the Police Headquarters in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: IDCOL, PRAN-RFL seek to drive green industrial growth
item.description: Infrastructure Development Company Limited and PRAN-RFL Group on Sunday showcased their strategic collaboration to accelerate energy efficiency and renewable energy adoption in Bangladesh’s industrial sector, said a press release......

item.title: United States farmers strained as fertiliser costs surge on war
item.description: On Andy Corriher’s farm in North Carolina, planting and preparations are underway for his corn and soybean crops — but fertiliser costs have surged on war in the Middle East, and orders he placed weeks ago have yet to arrive......

item.title: SBAC Bank deposits exceed Tk 11,000cr in 2025
item.description: SBAC Bank PLC has exceeded milestone of Tk 11,000 crore in deposits in 2025, achieving a growth of 15 per cent, said a press release......

item.title: Southeast Bank relocates Kawran Bazar branch
item.description: Southeast Bank PLC on Wednesday relocated its Kawran Bazar branch to a more spacious and modern location within the same building......

item.title: Islami Bank wins gold award for remittance services
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Sunday received ‘Gold Award’ presented by Centre for Non Resident Bangladeshi for its outstanding contribution to remittance services, said a press release......

item.title: NRBC Bank celebrates Bengali New Year 1433
item.description: NRBC Bank on Wednesday celebrated Bangla New Year 1433 on the occasion of Pahela Baishakh by holding events held at its head office as well as at all its branches and sub-branches nationwide, said a press release......

item.description: MTB Foundation recently inaugurated three water access hubs under project ‘Jol Torongo’ for the vulnerable communities of Kagabala, Prem Nagar and Satbagh of Moulvibazar District......

item.description: Ferdous Ara Begum has been elected as president of executive council of the Bangladesh Senior Citizens Forum for the term 2026-27, said a press release......

item.description: Far from the downturn that has shaken many luxury houses in recent years, Britain’s Rolls-Royce on Tuesday launched an ultra-luxury, limited-series electric car......

item.description: The forthcoming series against Bangladesh will be a huge opportunity for New Zealand’s next-in-line cricketers, according to their head coach Rob Walter......Politics analysis

item.description: Bangladesh are settled with their opening pair, stated head coach Phil Simmons on Wednesday ahead of the first ODI against New Zealand on April 17 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur......

item.description: The Bangladesh Table Tennis Federation on Wednesday appointed Japanese coach Yutaka Nakano for a two-year period ahead of the country’s paddlers’ campaign in the forthcoming five major international competitions......

item.title: Russia can ‘compensate’ for China’s resource gap from Iran war: Lavrov
item.description: Russia’s foreign minister said that his country could make up for China’s energy shortages as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is choked by the United States-Israeli war with Iran, Russian state media reported Wednesday......

item.title: Atletico ‘ready’ for CL success at last: Simeone
item.description: Atletico Madrid coach Diego Simeone said that his team was ready to finally win the Champions League after knocking out Barcelona on Tuesday to reach the semi-finals......

item.description: Former Real Madrid player and Spain’s 1982 World Cup coach Jose Emilio Santamaria has died at the age of 96, Los Blancos said on Wednesday......

item.title: Thirty coaches get level 3 certificate
item.description: Thirty coaches successfully completed the level 3 coaching course under the Bangladesh Cricket Board as they were handed their certificates at a ceremony held at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur on Wednesday......

item.title: FPWA demands removal of SAARC quota policy
item.description: The Football Players Welfare Association (FPWA) of Bangladesh demanded to scrap SAARC countries’ players’ local status policy to the Bangladesh Football Federation on Wednesday......

item.title: Dembele hails PSG’s ability to ‘suffer’
item.description: Double goal hero Ousmane Dembele hailed his Paris Saint-Germain teammates for holding on under pressure and in difficult conditions to beat Liverpool 2-0 at Anfield on Tuesday in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final and reach the last four with a 4-0 aggregate triumph......

item.description: New York-based news magazine TIME has named Bangladesh prime minister and also Bangladesh Nationalist Party chairman Tarique Rahman among its list of the world’s 100 most influential people in 2026......

item.description: New York-based news magazine TIME has named Bangladesh prime minister and also Bangladesh Nationalist Party chairman Tarique Rahman among its list of the world’s 100 most influential people in 2026....

item.description: Gaza’s civil defence agency said on Tuesday that a toddler was among 10 people killed in separate Israeli strikes in the northern part of the Palestinian territory......

item.title: Xi meets Russian FM as leaders flock to China over ME war
item.description: China’s president Xi Jinping met with Russian foreign minister Sergei Lavrov on Wednesday, Chinese state media said, as a string of leaders of countries impacted by the Middle East war flock to Beijing......

item.title: Student kills four wounds 20 in new Turkey school shooting
item.description: A student aged about 13 opened fire at random in a Turkish school Wednesday killing four people and wounding 20, just one day after a shooter wounded 16 people and then killed himself in another school, officials said......

item.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 stranded Iranian sailors
item.description: Sri Lanka has repatriated 238 Iranian sailors stranded in the South Asian country after one of their warships was torpedoed by a US submarine, a minister said on Wednesday......

item.title: Italy shifts away from Israel, US over Mideast war
item.description: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni received a sharp rebuke from US president Donald Trump and suspended a defence agreement with Israel on Tuesday, signalling a shift in her foreign relations......

item.title: New Hungarian leader says pro-Orban president must go
item.description: Hungary’s new leader Peter Magyar said Wednesday he had told President Tamas Sulyok, a supporter of ousted prime minister Viktor Orban, that he should resign as part of a clearout after Orban’s election defeat......

item.title: 331 children contracted HIV from syringe reuse in Pakistan hospital
item.description: An undercover investigation by BBC News has exposed systemic infection control failures at THQ Hospital Taunsa in Pakistan, where staff were filmed reusing syringes on children. The footage revealed dangerous hygiene violations and the reuse of equipment for multiple patients, contributing to a broader crisis of HIV outbreaks linked to unsafe medical practices......

item.title: North Korea boosting ability to make nuclear arms: UN
item.description: North Korea is showing a ‘very serious increase’ in its ability to produce atomic weapons, the head of the UN nuclear watchdog said during a visit to Seoul on Wednesday......

item.title: Iran FM welcomes Pakistan delegation led by army chief
item.description: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi welcomed on Wednesday a Pakistani delegation led by army chief Asim Munir, days after failed US-Iran talks in Islamabad to end the Middle East war....

item.title: Iran FM welcomes Pakistan delegation led by army chief
item.description: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi welcomed on Wednesday a Pakistani delegation led by army chief Asim Munir, days after failed US-Iran talks in Islamabad to end the Middle East war....

item.title: Bangladesh govt for ensuring wage board, fair pay for journalists, says minister
item.description: The Bangladesh information and broadcasting minister, Zahir Uddin Swapon, on Wednesday said the government is working to formulate a wage board and a structured pay scale for journalists through consultations with media owners to ensure fair salaries and professional dignity....

item.title: Fire at 11-storey building in Banani under control
item.description: A fire broke out at an 11-storey building in Banani, Dhaka, on Wednesday evening and was brought under control at around 8:15pm....

item.description: Iran said Wednesday that exchanges with the United States via Pakistan had continued following failed negotiations....

item.description: At least 11 students were injured and six vehicles damaged as a bus carrying students of Dhaka University hit other vehicles in Karwan Bazar area on Wednesday morning......

item.description: At least 11 students were injured and six vehicles damaged as a bus carrying students of Dhaka University hit other vehicles in Karwan Bazar area on Wednesday morning....

item.description: Bangladesh national team head coach Phil Simmons remained stoic with all the turmoil going on in the top brass of the Bangladesh Cricket Board, while on Wednesday he stated that it didn’t affect his team’s regular activities. ...Politics analysis

item.title: Iran threatens to shut down Red Sea
item.description: Iran’s military threatened on Wednesday to shut down Red Sea trade unless the United States lifted its naval blockade on Tehran’s ports, saying the ceasefire was at risk......

item.title: Iran warns will block Red Sea if US naval blockade continues
item.description: Iran’s military warned on Wednesday it would block trade through the Red Sea, along with the Gulf and Sea of Oman, if the US naval blockade continues....

item.title: Bangladesh govt adopts long-term plans to fight climate change: environment minister
item.description: Environment, forest and climate change minister Abdul Awal Mintoo on Wednesday told Parliament that the government had undertaken a series of comprehensive measures to protect the country from the adverse impacts of climate change......

item.title: Govt monitors fertiliser management with highest priority, says agriculture minister
item.description: Agriculture minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid on Wednesday told Parliament that there is no scope for selling fertilisers at higher prices as the government is closely monitoring fertiliser management with highest priority......

item.title: Govt to install 8,000 air-flow machines to cut onion storage losses
item.description: Agriculture minister Mohammad Aminur Rashid has announced plans to install a total of 8,000 air-flow machines in onion- and garlic-producing regions, including Rajbari district, in a bid to reduce post-harvest losses and improve storage capacity......
